sections.paragraph:
m., constr. קְצֵה, with suff. קָצֵהוּ; once plur. with suff. קְצֵיהֶם Eze 33:2.-

(1) i.q. קֵץ end, extremity-(a) of space, e.g. of the desert, Exo 13:20 of a camp, Num 11:1 of a region, Num 33:37 of a country, Isa 5:26 of heaven, Isa 13:5 Psa 19:7 Isa 7:18, בִּקְצֵה יְאֹרֵי מִצְרַיִם “in the extremity (i.e. on the bank, margin) of the rivers of Egypt.” Gen 19:4, “all the people מִקָּצֶה from the end,” i.e. all together; Gen 19:4 Jerem. 51:31 (compare Arabic عن اقصا; see Schult. Opp. Min. p. 121); compare No. 2.-(b) of time. Often in this connection, מִקְצֵה שְׁלשֶׁת יָמִים at the end of three days, after three days, Jos 3:2 9:16 Gen 8:3 1Ki 9:10 2Ki 8:3 18:10 Eze 39:14. The same as מִקֵּץ; see קֵץ .

(2) the whole, the sum. Gen 47:2, מִקְצֵה אֶחָיו “from the whole number of his brethren;” Eze 33:2. Comp. Num 22:41 Isa 56:11. See the origin of this signification in No. 1, letter a, fin.
